little Indian village to the hustling,
busy city of to-day--nay, more, he has
been a power from the first in this de-
velopment. Financial storms and the
great fire which burned nearly the
whole of Spokane have sought the I'M
doing of this veteran of the west, but
AS the ueen City of the Inland En-
pire arose, Phoenix-like, from it's ashes
SO has the splendid business acumen
of this pioneer prevailed stoutly over
all adversely. He is now the much re-
spected and well-loved citizen for whom
city additions are named-the president
of those substantial companies, the
Spokane Investment company and the
Columbia Investment company, and of
the Columbia Valley bank at Wenat-
chez, and of the Couer D'Alene Bank
and Trust company at Occur DAlenc,
Idaho. Any schoolboy in Eastern
Washington knows his name, or ought
to, but for the recent and unitiat ed
conner we will state that it is j. J.
Browne,
